judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent queried 2020 citations and paper texts, then in execute_python incorrectly identified CHI papers by searching only for the literal token "CHI" (regex r"\bCHI\b") in the first 4000 characters of each paper’s text—missing venues labeled as "SIGCHI" or spelled out—so it found none and summed to 0 instead of aggregating all CHI papers’ 2020 citations.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent determined CHI papers by querying MongoDB for any document whose text contains “CHI” (call_gzJ24rJa0EVzaBEoJMILKTjE) and summing 2020 citations for those titles (call_5vcq0oUf0arcv6OosiY6xJs8), a flawed plan that does not reliably identify the CHI venue and thus produced 3524 instead of the ground-truth 3835.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent implemented a correct plan (join 2020 citations with CHI papers) but used an overly narrow regex in execute_python to detect CHI in paper texts (e.g., matching only ASCII "CHI '##" and missing variants like curly quotes or other CHI phrasings), resulting in only 2 papers being labeled CHI and an undercount of 68 instead of the true 3835.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent prefiltered the paper_docs with a Mongo regex requiring the standalone token "CHI" (call_TN6wQ2rOLjmqxP2vq28WhaBv), thereby excluding CHI papers that only contain "SIGCHI"/"Proceedings of the SIGCHI..." and leading the subsequent join and sum in execute_python (call_EkXfcCr34OQn1CT88tXUy0Qd) to find no matches and return 0 instead of 3835.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: yes
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent computed CHI papers by scanning full text with regexes that omitted the curly apostrophe (after an earlier SyntaxError), so in the contributing execute_python call it failed to match many "CHI ’YY" headers and undercounted the set of CHI papers, yielding 3201 instead of 3835.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
